data.title: STOCKS | Guosheng Securities Warns of Potential Stock Market Adjustment Amid Rising Oil Prices
data.description:
On March 20, Guosheng Securities released a report indicating that the U.S. Federal Reserve may adopt a cautious stance in the short term due to increasing uncertainties, particularly focusing on changes in oil prices and the duration of high oil prices. According to Jin10, the report suggests that the situation in Iran has persisted for over three weeks without signs of rapid resolution, pointing to sustained high prices for crude oil, natural gas, and other energy sources.

The report outlines a scenario where rising oil prices lead to increased inflation, prompting the Federal Reserve to pause interest rate cuts or even consider hikes, thereby increasing the likelihood of stagflation. This situation is expected to gradually tighten global liquidity and continue to suppress market risk appetite. Guosheng Securities advises caution regarding the potential for a significant adjustment in the stock market.

data.title: Prediction market Kalshi has secured over $1 billion in new funding, valuing the company at $22 billion.
data.description: Prediction market platform Kalshi has completed a new funding round exceeding $1 billion, valuing the company at $22 billion, more than double its $11 billion valuation in its previous funding round last December. Sources familiar with the matter revealed that the round was led by Coatue Management. Kalshi currently has annualized revenue of $1.5 billion. Founded in 2018, Kalshi saw a surge in popularity after a court granted it permission to offer election trading in 2024. The company operates as a CFTC-regulated financial exchange, allowing it to conduct business nationwide under federal rules. Its trading volume surpassed $10 billion in February, a 12-fold increase from six months prior. Despite recent criminal lawsuits and regulatory controversies in states like Arizona, investors remain enthusiastic about the rapidly growing market. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Seoul Police Uncover Major Money Laundering Operation Involving Family Members
data.description:
Seoul's Jungnang Police Station has dismantled a money laundering ring primarily composed of family members and relatives. According to ChainCatcher, the group operated an unregistered virtual asset exchange in Seoul's Myeongdong district, converting cash obtained from phone scams into Tether (USDT) and transferring it overseas. The laundering operation is suspected to involve several billion Korean won. Additionally, the group exploited rising precious metal prices by purchasing gold through cross-border virtual asset transactions and exporting it for profit.

Authorities have arrested 19 suspects, with four individuals, including the main money laundering suspect, identified as A, aged 46, currently in custody. The head of the fraud organization, identified as B, aged 44, has fled abroad, and police are actively pursuing him. The investigation has so far seized criminal proceeds, including cash, silver balls, and gold bars, totaling approximately 6 billion Korean won.

data.title: KOGAS Reports LNG Stock Levels Exceed Legal Reserve Requirements
data.description: South Korea's state-run Korea Gas Corporation (KOGAS) has announced that its liquefied natural gas (LNG) inventory levels have surpassed the legal reserve requirements. According to Jin10, this development comes as the company continues to manage its resources to ensure a stable supply of natural gas. KOGAS, a major player in the global LNG market, is responsible for importing and distributing natural gas across South Korea. The company’s adherence to maintaining higher-than-required reserves is seen as a strategic move to mitigate potential supply disruptions and to meet the country's energy demands efficiently. This announcement underscores KOGAS's commitment to energy security and its proactive approach in resource management.

data.title: AI TRENDS | China's Internet Control May Influence AI's Future, New Book Suggests
data.description:
China's internet has historically been a space of partial freedom and control, a dynamic that a new book suggests could influence the future of artificial intelligence. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the book's argument that the tension between control and freedom in China's digital landscape may play a significant role in shaping AI development.

The book delves into how China's approach to internet governance, characterized by a blend of regulation and openness, might impact AI technologies. This duality has allowed for innovation while maintaining governmental oversight, a balance that could extend to AI.

As AI continues to evolve, the book posits that China's model of internet control could serve as a framework for managing AI's growth and integration into society. The implications of this approach could be far-reaching, affecting global AI policies and practices.

The discussion around China's internet and AI highlights the broader debate on how nations can balance technological advancement with regulatory measures. The book's insights contribute to understanding the potential pathways AI development might take under varying governance models.

data.title: DDC Enterprise increased its holdings by 200 bitcoins, bringing its total bitcoin holdings to 2,383.
data.description: Publicly traded DDC Enterprise added 200 bitcoins to its holdings, bringing its total bitcoin holdings to 2,383, worth approximately $165 million. The company added about 200 bitcoins per week in January and about 100 bitcoins per week in February. DDC stated that the average price of the newly purchased bitcoins was $79,969. The company also reported a year-to-date “Bitcoin yield” of 44.9%, a company-defined metric typically used to track changes in bitcoin per share rather than traditional financial performance. This move places DDC 32nd among publicly traded corporate bitcoin holders, but still far behind large financial institutions holding tens of thousands of bitcoins. (The Block)

data.title: OpenAI plans to merge ChatGPT, Codex, and its browser into a desktop super app.
data.description: According to the WSJ, OpenAI plans to merge ChatGPT, Codex, and their browser into a single desktop "super app" to simplify the user experience.

data.title: Sexual Abuse Allegations Surface Against Civil Rights Leader César Chávez
data.description: Dolores Huerta and several other women have come forward with sexual abuse allegations against César Chávez, a prominent civil rights leader. Bloomberg posted on X that these claims have led to a rapid response in California, where Chávez's legacy is deeply rooted. The allegations have sparked discussions about the impact on Chávez's reputation and the broader civil rights movement. Authorities are reportedly looking into the matter, although no official statements have been released yet. The situation remains under scrutiny as more details emerge.

data.title: Bitcoin's Recent Rise Attributed to Short-Term Investor Activity
data.description: Bitcoin has experienced a 6% increase over the past month, according to NS3.AI. A recent research report indicates that this upward movement is primarily driven by short-term investors. Meanwhile, long-term holders have maintained a relatively stable accumulation pattern, suggesting a divergence in investment strategies between the two groups.

data.title: Amazon Experiences Issues in the U.S., Reports DownDetector
data.description: Amazon users in the United States have reported experiencing issues with the platform, according to DownDetector. The website, which monitors network conditions, has received numerous reports indicating problems with Amazon's services. The nature and extent of these issues remain unclear, and it is not yet known how many users are affected. Amazon has not released an official statement regarding the situation. Users are advised to stay updated through official channels for further information.

data.title: Security Operation in Mexico's Sinaloa State Results in 11 Deaths
data.description: A navy-led security operation in Mexico's Sinaloa state has resulted in the deaths of 11 individuals. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing efforts by Mexican authorities to combat organized crime in the region. The operation targeted one of the country's most influential cartels, reflecting the government's continued crackdown on drug-related activities. The incident underscores the persistent challenges faced by law enforcement in addressing cartel violence and maintaining security in affected areas.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Blue Origin Seeks FCC Approval for AI-Enabled Satellite Deployment
data.description: Blue Origin, the aerospace company founded by Jeff Bezos, is seeking permission from the U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to deploy nearly 52,000 satellites capable of artificial intelligence computing. According to Jin10, the Wall Street Journal reported that the company has filed documents with the FCC outlining its ambitious plan to enhance satellite-based AI capabilities. This move is part of a broader strategy to expand Blue Origin's presence in the space technology sector, leveraging AI to improve satellite functionality and data processing. The deployment of these satellites could significantly impact global communications and data management, offering advanced AI-driven solutions from orbit.

data.title: EU Commission President to Address Industry Concerns on Carbon Emissions Trading System
data.description: The European Union is set to review industry concerns regarding its carbon emissions trading system, according to European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen. According to Jin10, von der Leyen emphasized the importance of addressing these concerns to ensure the system's effectiveness and fairness. The carbon emissions trading system is a key component of the EU's strategy to combat climate change by reducing greenhouse gas emissions. The system has faced criticism from various industry sectors, which argue that it imposes significant financial burdens. Von der Leyen's statement indicates a willingness to engage with stakeholders to refine the system and address these challenges. The EU aims to balance environmental goals with economic considerations, ensuring that the transition to a low-carbon economy is both sustainable and equitable.

data.title: EU Commission President Calls for Modernization of Carbon Pricing Mechanism
data.description: European Union Commission President Ursula von der Leyen has emphasized the need to modernize the EU's carbon pricing mechanism. According to Jin10, von der Leyen highlighted the importance of updating the system to better address current environmental challenges and ensure effective climate action. The modernization aims to enhance the EU's ability to meet its climate goals and support sustainable economic growth. Von der Leyen's statement underscores the EU's commitment to leading global efforts in combating climate change and transitioning to a greener economy.

data.title: Anchorage Digital Expands Atlas Platform to Include Collateral Management Services
data.description: Anchorage Digital has announced the expansion of its Atlas platform to offer collateral management and oversight services for institutional clients. According to BlockBeats, the platform will cater to institutional lenders and credit providers, offering 24/7 real-time collateral monitoring and automated margin call notifications. These services are designed for a wide range of digital asset-backed transactions, including secured loans, convertible bonds, warehouse financing, structured products, OTC derivatives, equipment purchases, and PIPE financing.

data.title: Bitcoin Transfer to Jump Crypto Observed
data.description: At 07:03, a total of 49.4 BTC was transferred from several anonymous addresses to Jump Crypto. According to ChainCatcher, this transaction was tracked using Arkham data, highlighting the movement of significant Bitcoin assets. The transfer underscores the ongoing activity within the cryptocurrency market, where large transactions are frequently monitored for insights into market trends and behaviors.

data.title: Survey Reveals Low Trust in Political Leaders Among Federal Workers
data.description: A recent survey conducted by the Partnership for Public Service indicates that only 10% of federal employees who participated expressed confidence in their agencies' political leaders. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the survey's findings, which underscore a significant trust deficit within federal institutions. The survey results suggest a need for improved leadership and communication within these agencies to enhance trust and morale among federal workers.

data.title: Studio Dominates Academy Awards with Top Films
data.description: Bloomberg posted on X that the studio responsible for distributing the two films that won the most Academy Awards this year has emerged as a dominant force in the industry. The studio's success highlights its strategic prowess in selecting and promoting films that resonate with both critics and audiences. This achievement underscores the studio's influential role in shaping cinematic trends and its ability to consistently deliver award-winning content. The recognition at the Academy Awards further solidifies the studio's reputation as a leader in the film distribution sector.

data.title: AI Technology Diversion to China Leads to Charges in New York
data.description: Three individuals have been charged in New York for allegedly attempting to illegally transfer billions of dollars' worth of artificial intelligence technology from a U.S.-based manufacturer to China. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the seriousness of the charges, which underscore ongoing concerns about technology theft and national security. The case reflects broader tensions between the U.S. and China over technological advancements and intellectual property rights. Authorities are intensifying efforts to prevent unauthorized technology transfers that could compromise national interests.

data.title: Crypto Market Decline Continues as Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady
data.description: The cryptocurrency market experienced further declines, with the total market capitalization decreasing by 1.5% to $2.48 trillion. According to NS3.AI, this downturn followed the Federal Reserve's decision to maintain interest rates and raise its 2026 PCE inflation forecast. Bitcoin's price hovered around $70,000, marking a 1.3% drop over the past 24 hours. Meanwhile, Ethereum (ETH) and Solana (SOL) both saw a 2% decrease. In the same period, approximately 118,000 leveraged traders faced liquidations amounting to $405 million. Additionally, Bitcoin ETFs witnessed $163.5 million in outflows on Tuesday, breaking a seven-day streak of inflows.
